1990_01_12-India-Mumbai-SB-7_13_45_Spiritual-Progress-st1-vr01

Start Lecture Transcribe:

svātma-vṛttaṁ mayetthaṁ te

suguptam api varṇitam

vyapetaṁ loka-śāstrābhyāṁ

bhavān hi bhagavat-paraḥ

[Sb 7.13.45]

TRANSLATION

Prahlāda Mahārāja, you are certainly a self-realized soul and a devotee of the Supreme Lord. You do not care for public opinion or so-called scriptures. For this reason I have described to you without hesitation the history of my self-realization.

PURPORT

A person who is actually a devotee of Kṛṣṇa does not care about so-called public opinion and Vedic or philosophical literatures. Prahlāda Mahārāja, who is such a devotee, always defied the false instructions of his father and the so-called teachers who were appointed to teach him. Instead, he simply followed the instructions of Nārada Muni, his guru, and thus he always remained a stalwart devotee. This is the nature of an intelligent devotee. The Srimad-Bhāgavatam instructs, yajnaih sankirtana-prāyair yajanti hi sumedhasah [Sb 11.5.32]. One who is actually very intelligent must join the Krsna consciousness movement, realizing his own self as an eternal servant of Krsna, and thus practice constant chanting of the holy name of the Lord—Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare/Hare Rāma, Hare Rāma, Rāma Rāma, Hare Hare.

Start Lecture by His Holiness Radhanath Swami Maharaja.

A learned thoughtful person must realize that material existence is illusion. This is only possible by self realization. A self realized person, who has actually seen the truth, should retire from all material activities, being situated in self realization. Prahlāda Mahārāja, you are certainly a self-realized soul and a devotee of the Supreme Lord. You do not care for public opinion or so-called scriptures. For this reason I have described to you without hesitation the history of my self-realization. Sometimes, it is said, it takes one to know one. We have many shastric references which explains the qualities, attributes of the self realized soul. And if we are sincere due to our great desire to approach Krishna,

īśvaraḥ sarva-bhūtānāṁ

hṛd-deśe 'rjuna tiṣṭhati

bhrāmayan sarva-bhūtāni

yantrārūḍhāni māyayā

[Bg 18.61]

[The Supreme Lord is situated in everyone's heart, O Arjuna, and is directing the wanderings of all living entities, who are seated as on a machine, made of the material energy.]

Krishna within the heart, directs us to the great soul, to their lotus feet. But factually the only person, who could truly know the real qualities of self realized soul, is a self realized soul. We may study the shastra and get all the information of who is a self realized soul. But factually it is only by the causeless mercy of Krishna that we can ever attain the lotus feet of such a great personality. It is not due to our understanding the qualities. But if we do not strive to understand the qualities then Krishna will not take our sincerity very seriously. So, therefore, it is only by the causeless mercy of Krishna that we attain guru. And it is only by the causeless mercy of guru that we attain Krishna. Sometimes people ask, so many people have so many interpretations of what a self realized soul is, and so many people have so many interpretations of what the scripture says is, self realized soul is, so who to believe. Ultimately, Krishna describes that one should receive his information, if he wants to be guaranteed, through the parampara system of the Lord. But ultimately, it is only by the mercy of Krishna. He is the source of all of our intelligence. But in truth it takes one to know one. One who is actually seeing Krishna can understand in truth, who else is seeing Krishna. So here this great brāhmaṇa, Avadhuta he understands due to his own self realization, he understands through realization the glorious infinite qualities of Prahlada Maharaja. And he is speaking here very deeply. Notice how his praise of is not based on some superficial characteristics that Prahalad Maharaja may possess. He is explaining to Prahalada that you are a self realized soul and you are a true devotee of Lord. Why? Because you have no false ego! Because you could not care less about public opinion and you could not care less about the various apparent contradiction of the scriptures. You are simply fixed in the service of your spiritual master and you are simply fixed in the glorification of Sri Krishna and this is really the criterion that the advanced devotees see in others. We have been explaining this principle very often in our classes, because it is the most important principle. We have to understand what Krishna Consciousness is and what Krishna Consciousness is not. There is [7:49] very few devotees, who even understand this. Generally, because we are so externally oriented, we consider a person’s material qualifications to be a sign of spiritual advancement. But factually material qualifications have nothing to do with spiritual advancement. Spiritual advancement is being explained by the Avadhuta brāhmaṇa in this verse. Spiritual advancement is…[8:38]………. A neophyte person sees oh! This person is very good singer, he must be so advanced. But there are so many good singers, who are completely puppets in the hands of maya. Or someone may think, oh! This person is such good cook, must be very spiritually advanced. But how many good cooks are there at Tajmahal Hotel. They don’t even know ‘what is Krishna?’ or ‘Who is Krishna?’. They are simply cooking for money. Or someone is thinking, oh! This person is so brilliant in intelligence, must be very spiritual advanced. But how many professors and how many politicians are manipulating the world with their intelligence. And many of them are demoniac, what to speak of spiritual advancement? And another may think, Oh! This person is so successful; this must be a sign of his spiritual advancement. But by material standards how many successful people are there in America, in Europe, and they are all addicted to all sinful activities. Oh! This person ah, he is so pious; he must be very spiritually advanced. But piety is simply a mode of goodness. Mode of goodness leads to another birth in this material world. So you see, immature people, they are considering devotees by their material qualification, to be very advanced and they take shelter of such persons. But to the degree a devotee actually is spiritually advanced, to the [10:47] degree a person captures what is the essence of self realization, he confirms what this great soul, is here, is expressing. Because he has seen and considered the whole world; this man, he has seen all material qualifications and he has completely rejected them all. The Avadhuta brahmana is describing how he has gone through the cities, he has gone through the towns, he has gone through the villages, he has gone through the agricultural fields, he has lived in the forest, he has lived in the jungles, he has seen everything, and he has tried everything. By performing tapasya, austerities he has challenged his own false ego, he has challenged his senses and his mind, all the external coverings that are inflicted upon this soul and his realization is “Prahalad, I adore you, and I worship you, and I love you”. Because, I know, you are a self realized soul. Because you do not care for public opinion or so called contradiction of the scriptures. In another words because Prahalad you have no false ego. Although Lord Narashinga deva personally incarnated just to protect you, and although Lord Narahari Himself, personally put you on the throne of the universe, and although Brahma and Siva and Indra and Vayu, Surya, and Varuna, and Candra - all the greatest, most exulted personalities in the entire universe - they all came to worship you and perform the auspicious ceremony to make you the King of the universe, you never considered yourself to have any qualifications, whatsoever. You always considered yourself to be the most insignificant unworthy servant of the servant of the servant of the servant. From the bottom of your heart you are considering everything, the mercy of God. You never expected anything from anyone. You are always the humble servant of all living creatures. Because you have that qualification, I see you as spiritually advanced. Without that qualification, whatever else you have, a great soul like this brāhmaṇa will not take you very seriously, spiritually. How can it[13:59]? Everything else is coming and going, coming and going, coming and going, but the desire to be the servant of the servant of the servant of the servant is our eternal constitutional position. So this quality of Prahlada which is most exalted of all his qualities is here being glorified by the Avadhuta brāhmaṇa. And we must understand what it is that we should be looking for, when we want to take shelter of a devotee of the Lord. Then we should also know very clearly, what it is within ourselves that has to be purified and rectified. What is the real goal of our spiritual disciplines in life? To become the servant of the servant it is not to become great. A devotee does not want to become great. The devotee wants to make Krishna great. A devotee wants to make his spiritual master known for their greatness. That’s all. The devotee is empty of any desire for his own aggrandizement. And therefore Krishna will always test His devotee. And those tests for the advanced devotees, they only glorify their greatness and for a neophyte devotee those very tests give him a proper understanding of what Krishna Consciousness should be. Every morning we pray,

vāñchā-kalpatarubhyaś ca

kṛpā-sindhubhya eva ca,

patitānāṁ pāvanebhyo

vaiṣṇavebhyo namo namaḥ

The devotees are like the desire trees, because they fulfill all our desires, they are full of compassion for the conditioned souls. It is Krishna through the devotee that is, showing his compassion. So sometimes we think, why did, why is this devotee dealing with me in this way? Ah, the fact is, if he is a vaisnava, he is simply an instrument in Krishna to purify your heart. That’s why he is dealing with you in that way, it is not him, its Krishna. He is a desire tree. If your desire to become Krishna Consciousness, he is come to fulfill your desire. Try to understand Prabhupada’s position. Here he was, at the age of seventy he traveled all the way to the United States of America simply to fulfill the order his spiritual master, because his guru maharaja had such a burning desire that the people of the west be given a chance to understand Lord Caitanya’s mission. So he risked his life, he risked his everything; he accepted such hardships and inconvenience to go there. And then when he finally got there, ah, he saw that this is such a degraded civilization, everyone is utterly stuck in the modes of passion and ignorance. How I am possibly going to bring them to the platform of becoming a pure vaisnavas? How am I going to possibly give them understanding of brahmanical culture? Therefore he had to adjust many rules and regulations to give Krishna Consciousness to these people. He gave brahmanical initiation to those who are born in the families of mlecchas and yavans. He even gave sannyas initiation to such persons. And when he came back to India, of course many people could understand his glory and they were giving him great respect, but there was another class of men who simply had nothing but the stones of blasphemy to throw at him. Saying “what is this nonsense man, he is ruining the Vedic culture, he is initiating the mlecchas and yavans with the sacred thread. He is giving the Holy Name to people who are undeserving. He is giving sannyas to people who are not born in brahmana families. Not only that, he is allowing men to live in the same building with women. Not only that but he is performing marriage ceremonies as a sannyasi. They had nothing but criticism for him. What to speak of all the untrue things they said about him? And Prabhupada said in this regard, He said “the dogs would bark but the caravan will carry on”. He did not care. He prayed for the soul of these unfortunate persons. He was undisturbed. Lord, Jesus Christ and His followers, my god, what they said about them? This is a fact, this is in history books. We have seen. When Jesus Christ, original apostles when they would have meetings. There was a class of priests, who were against them. They would say that they are coming together to have illicit sex and horrible, horrible parties based on horrible sinful activities. Ah, and people in general said [20:20], what! They are doing like that. But the sincere, Krishna will protect. So this is historically, always the case. Therefore, a devotee who is disturbed by true or untrue things that is said about him, according to the Avadhuta brāhmaṇa, is not very much to be taken, seriously, as an advanced devotee. A devotee is simply fixed in glorifying Krishna. A devotee will give his life to defend another vaisnava. But he won’t even give a second to defend himself. Because a devotee knows that as soon as pride enters into his heart nothing he does has any flavor to Krishna. In this story this principle is very nicely illustrated in Madhya Lila of Caitanya-caritamrita, where Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu is traveling to South-India. And he was staying at the home of the Kurma brāhmaṇa. Now he stayed there for several days and some distance away there was a devotee who was suffering due to a severe infliction of leprosy. This devotee was very anxious to just get a moment’s darshan with Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu. And so he came to where Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu was. When he got to the place of kurma kshetra he found that Caitanya Mahaprabhu had already left. So he was feeling greatly unfortunate, due to his sinful activities he had missed this opportunity. But of course, please understand, it is not by our own material attempts that we can attain the darshan of Krishna or his great souls. It is only by the causeless mercy. Lord Caitanya knew the heart of this devotee and therefore he reversed his travels and came back to that place. This devotee was so humble that he did not consider anything of himself. He only considered how he could serve others. Even the most least and insignificant creatures, he put those living entities before himself in every respect. And how did he show this? It is easy to talk about it, but to actually perform sacrifice for the sake of others, that is the test. Anybody can give nice dissertations describing how we should be very compassionate and we should be very selfless. But who is actually willing to do it. Who is actually willing to suffer to help another? The kurma brāhmaṇa, due to his advanced condition of leprosy, he had just uncountable worms eating away his body. And when one of those worms would fall to the ground the Kurma brāhmaṇa would think so much compassion to that worm, that how will he survive, how will he eat? So he would pick up that worm with great care and place it back in his body. Thinking, this body is not meant for me, this body is meant for you, this body is meant for you to enjoy, so come back. This was to the degree he was compassionate to the least and most insignificant creatures, and he was willing to suffer and sacrifice his very life for a worm. Ah, we are talking about true story. This is the reality of consciousness of such great soul is the extent of their humility. So when he came to meet with Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, he felt oh my lord, you are so kind. I do not deserve to have your association. But you are just so merciful, patita pavana even to the most fallen, look at how sinful I am. And he began to offer beautiful prayers to Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu. And then Lord Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu went to embrace him and [the kurma brahmana was feeling very ashamed, (I am sorry)] Vasudev was feeling very ashamed. How can the lord embrace me with this body, which is full of sores and full of pus and full of blood and full of worms and full of disease? But you see Krishna does not see the external appearance of anything. Krishna only sees the humble devotion. Therefore he embraced the body of Vasudeva and instantly upon the touch of Lord all of his leprosy vanished. And his deformed horrible body became munificent and effulgent like that of a demigod. Now you would think when you are suffering in such a condition, obviously very close on the path to death, if you are healed you are going to feel very happy. Would you not? Just like, many times we have seen, in the west there are Christian healers and they just they actually have mystic powers, and people who have had diseases for long long times and suffering for long times, right on the stage bring them up on the stage and they say “Lord heal this person” and then the person who’s had, just throws his crutches away and starts dancing. And that person is so happy, haaa just see the Lord has healed me, now I am again, I can work, I can play, I can do everything, very happy. So when a person in a desperate miserable suffering condition and a relief from that condition, generally they feel very grateful and very happy. The kurma brāhmaṇa was in the most extreme condition of suffering. And when he was healed he did not feel happy. He felt afraid. He said, “My dear Lord just see the mercy I have received from you”. You have healed me form such a disease. Now what’s going to happen? Is I am going to become famous. Every where I go people are going to recognize me as vasudeva amrita prada - Vasudev who received the life giving nectar of Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, who is the recipient of such special mercy of the Lord. Now my lord if I start thinking I am special to you, then I am going to get proud. And if pride enters my heart, for any reason, then my entire service to you is spoiled, it’s meaningless. It’s useless. I would rather be suffering and inflicted by being eaten by millions of worms in a condition near death with leprosy. Understanding my humble position and offer me service than to have a beautiful golden body being praised by others and being proud that I am an advanced devotee. So my Lord with tears in my eyes I am praying how in this condition of receiving such special benediction form you, can I be free from pride which will ruin everything. Then Sri Caitanya Mahaprabhu, he told him that you always chant the holy names, “Hare Krishna Hare Krishna, Krishna Krishna, Hare Hare Hare Rama Hare Rama, Rama Rama, Hare Hare”. And who ever you meet you be their humble servant by telling them about Krishna consciousness. Through this humble service you will never be inflicted by the greatest enemy, the greatest disease of pride. See, leprosy is not a very serious disease. Why? Because it is simply something that temporarily inconveniences your body. But pride is the most serious disease, because it infects the very essence of the existence of the soul. It keeps you……. (30:40) from Krishna. So therefore, those personalities who have overcome this great obstacle they are worshipable by all living beings. And Krishna, so kindly, will put us in predicaments to facilitate, first of all, we see how much pride is within our heart. And he also puts us in that situation so that we can have the opportunity to overcome it by becoming the humble servant in that predicament. Another most illustrious example was Lord Nityananda. When Jagai and Madai were approached by him, they simply insulted him. They were saying the most horrible blasphemous words about him. Not only verbally but they considered him such a low class fool that they took a pot and smashed his head with it, a blow that should have killed him. Lord Nityananda did not feel that they had committed any offence to him. In fact, Lord Nityananda was simply praying for their salvation. But Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu, he was very angry. He was ready to kill them. He called the Sudarshan Chakra and with eyes red like hot coals, he was about to give them their ticket to the abode of Yamraja. But Lord Nityananda said, “no, no my Lord please be kind upon them, give them mercy, forgive them, as I have forgiven them”. This is the quality of the great souls. They can accept any amount of personal abuse but they cannot see, they cannot tolerate seeing another devotee abused. Of course a neophyte devotee, when he is abused he becomes very offended and starts abusing others, even if they are devotees. That is the tendency within all of us, is it not? We feel ourselves abused, so we just want to abuse others, and we feel perfectly justified for doing so. This is not the way Lord Nityananda taught us. He was willing to accept the worst abuse and simply pray for the welfare and be the servant of those who abused him. But that same Lord Nityanda and Caitanya Mahaprabhu also, Caitanya Mahaprabhu, Ramachandra Puri came to Jagannath Puri and he simply made up so many lies about Lord Caitanya, untrue things. He said, Lord Caitanya was simply overeating, he was a sense enjoyer, he was a bogus useless sannyasi ah and he would just take things out of context. People would cook for Lord Caitanya, Lord Caitanya although he was a sannyasi, he thought I have to reciprocate with my devotees. I have to accept their offerings. Although I am a sannyasi, maybe I shouldn’t have! I have to do it. So they would cook for him, they were so anxious to give him their love through their preparations and he would go there and he would eat, out of humility, out of service, out of compassion for them. And this Ramacandra Puri came in and said “nonsense, useless sannyasi look he is eating opulent food; Sannyasi should only eat just remnant of rice. So Lord Caitanya said alright from now you just give me a hand full of rice. And even after that one day he saw some ants in His room. He said “just see these nonsense ants!” Basically a fault finder will just find a slightest excuse to confirm that this person is a rascal. And Lord Caitanya Mahaprabhu, his devotees, they were just ready to do just anything to this Ramachandra puri. Lord Caitanya said, “No no no no he is my senior, he is my, I am his servant, he is a respectable person, we must offer him all respect” And at the same time Caitanya Mahaprabhu was just very humble, yes! Yes! I agree, you are correct from now on, only this much, he would never even defend himself hardly, but when someone criticized his devotee, his eyes were like red fire and he took his Sudarshan chakra and was ready to kill them. Ah, this is because he was really the servant of the servant. He had no false pride. He was willing to accept all abuse like a tree.

tṛṇād api sunīcena

taror api sahiṣṇunā

amāninā mānadena

kīrtanīyaḥ sadā hariḥ

Shikshaktakam 3/ Cc Adi 17.31

[One who thinks himself lower than the grass, who is more tolerant than a tree, and who does not expect personal honor yet is always prepared to give all respect to others can very easily always chant the holy name of the Lord]

Caitanya Mahaprabhu has taught us that we should expect absolutely no respect for oneself but offer all respect to all others, according to what they deserve. This is a most the important instruction that Caitanya Mahaprabhu has given. He claims that this verse is the most important of all verses. To be more humble than the grass; to be more tolerant than the tree; to offer all respect to others and to expect none for oneself. So we have to understand that this is the real Krishna Consciousness [37] meaning is. And we have to evaluate in our own selves this is the real qualifications that makes my service pleasing to Krishna. And in others we have to understand that although I am a servant of all vaishnavas, the real vaishnavas, who are worshipable and advanced, are not, could be the simple illiterate sweeper of the temple floor. If he has these qualities, he is most advanced. But if he has a big big position with power and control but he doesn’t have these qualities, I will offer him all respect as a vaishnava. But I will not take very seriously his spiritual advancement. Because spiritual advancement means, being the humble servant of the servant of the servant of the servant. There is no other qualification that is pleasing to Krishna. Everything else is like so many zeros. Unless that humble service attitude which is the ‘ONE’ that is placed before the zeros, then all those zeros have no meaning and no value to Krishna, or to the advanced devotees who are his servants.

So the verse today we are reading explains, “Prahlāda Mahārāja, you are certainly a self-realized soul and a devotee of the Supreme Lord. You do not care for public opinion or so-called scriptures. For this reason I have described to you without hesitation the history of my self-realization”. We see because this great brāhmaṇa could see the glorious humility in Prahlada Maharaja, there was naturally an intimacy. And in that intimacy he felt very free to reveal the most confidential thoughts within his heart, to his friend. And actually the spiritual master, he reveals the true essence of Krishna Consciousness to his disciples, to the degree his disciple is submissive.

End of the transcription.
